comments.comment: Carbon monoxide mass flowrates in source tests ranged from 24.89 kg/hr to 1726.34 kg/hr in 9 runs throughout the year for stack 1 and 5.36 kg/hr to 534.98 for stack 2, which is a function of the amount of excess air in the combustion chamber and the reaction of steam and carbon dioxide. This variability leads to large year to year variances in reported CO

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Began use of an Activated Carbon Injection system on all 3 units therefore Mercury is being captured in the fly ash instead of being emitted.
Capture rate of Mercury in 2011 was above 74% on both stacks.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
increase in stack release due to operational variability.
decrease in road dust due to, 35% increase in day with precipitation resulting in significantly less dust.
There was also less dust to be returned to the mine.

No change in stack release.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
PM from stack releases is within 10%(3%)
For road dust (10.8%) difference, there were fewer days in 2010 with rain or snow cover, resulting in more dust releases.
for storage and handling emissions (-14.1% difference) there was less fly ash returned to the mine in 2010 compared to 2009, resulting in less fugitive emissions.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
PM from stack releases is within 10% (3% change)
For road dust (10.8% difference), there were less days in 2010 with rain or snow cover, resulting in more dust releases
For storage/handling emissions (-14.1% difference), there was less Fly Ash returned to the mine in 2010 compared to 2009, resulting in less fugitive emissions.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
PM from stack release is within 10% (3% change)
For road dust (10.8% difference) there were less days in 2010 with rain or snow cover, resulting in more dust releases.
For storage/handling emissions (-14.1% difference), there was less fly ash returned to the mine in 2010 compared to 2009, resulting in less fugitive emissions.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Further review of PM emissions reported determined that an update was needed.  Previously stack tests from 2019 were used to determine PM2.5 and PM10 emissions and more recent (2021) stack tests were used for TPM.  This resulted in TPM being less than PM10 so PM10 was used for TPM.  However since the PM2.5 and PM10 stack tests were older, it was determined that it was more appropriate to use the more recent TPM stack test results and the PM size distribution ration for coal power generation.  This update reflects the change in PM emissions using TPM stack tests from 2021 and PM size distribution ratios.

Lower road dust emissions due to removing out of service vehicles from the analysis

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Further review of PM emissions reported determined that an update was needed.  Previously stack tests from 2019 were used to determine PM2.5 and PM10 emissions and more recent (2021) stack tests were used for TPM.  This resulted in TPM being less than PM10 so PM10 was used for TPM.  However since the PM2.5 and PM10 stack tests were older, it was determined that it was more appropriate to use the more recent TPM stack test results and the PM size distribution ration for coal power generation.  This update reflects the change in PM emissions using TPM stack tests from 2021 and PM size distribution ratios.

comments.comment: Due to an increase in the use of natural gas for electrical generation and a decrease in the use of coal, the total on-site releases to air decreased from 2021 to 2022. Water release emissions increased in 2022 compared to 2021 due to an increase in water discharge. Water intake and discharge volumes are used to control cooling pond levels/conditions, and do not reflect changes in operations.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: Unit G2 was out of operation in 2021, which resulted in lower generation (i.e. less coal burned) and emissions from the site. In 2022, unit G2 was placed back into operation, resulting in an increase in coal burned for electrical generation that increased the off-site disposal of ash for the 2022 reporting year.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Air emissions increased due to an increase in generation from the units going to stack 1. Water release emissions increased in 2022 compared to 2021 due to an increase in water discharge.  Water intake and discharge volumes are used to control cooling pond levels/conditions, and do not reflect changes in operations.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: Unit G2 was out of operation in 2021, which resulted in lower generation (i.e. less coal burned) and emissions from the site. In 2022, unit G2 was placed back into operation, resulting in an increase in coal burned for electrical generation that increased the off-site disposal of ash for the 2022 reporting year.
